title: sambucus nigra var. canadensis
intro: Sambucus nigra var. canadensis, also known as American Black Elderberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, belonging to the Adoxaceae family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of American Black Elderberry include reducing the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and sore throats. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
